name: goshs
title: goshs

commands.name: goshs

arguments.description: --ip             IP or Interface to listen on              (default: 0.0.0.0)
arguments.raw: -i,  --ip             IP or Interface to listen on              (default: 0.0.0.0)

arguments.description: --port           The port to listen on                     (default: 8000)
arguments.raw: -p,  --port           The port to listen on                     (default: 8000)

arguments.description: --dir            The web root directory                    (default: current working path)
arguments.raw: -d,  --dir            The web root directory                    (default: current working path)

arguments.description: --webdav         Also serve using webdav protocol          (default: false)
arguments.raw: -w,  --webdav         Also serve using webdav protocol          (default: false)

arguments.description: The port to listen on for webdav          (default: 8001)
arguments.raw: -wp, --webdav-port    The port to listen on for webdav          (default: 8001)

arguments.description: Read only mode, no upload possible        (default: false)
arguments.raw: -ro, --read-only      Read only mode, no upload possible        (default: false)

arguments.description: Upload only mode, no download possible    (default: false)
arguments.raw: -uo, --upload-only    Upload only mode, no download possible    (default: false)

arguments.description: Specify a different upload folder         (default: current working path)
arguments.raw: -uf, --upload-folder  Specify a different upload folder         (default: current working path)

arguments.description: Maximum upload size in bytes, 0=unlimited (default: 0)
arguments.raw: -mu, --max-upload     Maximum upload size in bytes, 0=unlimited (default: 0)

arguments.description: Disable the clipboard sharing             (default: false)
arguments.raw: -nc, --no-clipboard   Disable the clipboard sharing             (default: false)

arguments.description: Disable the delete option                 (default: false)
arguments.raw: -nd, --no-delete      Disable the delete option                 (default: false)

arguments.description: Running without dir listing               (default: false)
arguments.raw: -si, --silent         Running without dir listing               (default: false)

arguments.description: --invisible      Invisible mode                            (default: false)
arguments.raw: -I,  --invisible      Invisible mode                            (default: false)

arguments.description: --cli            Enable cli (only with auth and tls)       (default: false)
arguments.raw: -c,  --cli            Enable cli (only with auth and tls)       (default: false)

arguments.description: Enable reverse shell catcher              (default: false)
arguments.raw: --catcher, -rc        Enable reverse shell catcher              (default: false)

arguments.description: --embedded       Show embedded files in UI                 (default: false)
arguments.raw: -e,  --embedded       Show embedded files in UI                 (default: false)

arguments.description: --output         Write output to logfile                   (default: false)
arguments.raw: -o,  --output         Write output to logfile                   (default: false)

arguments.description: --tunnel         Enable tunnel                             (default: false)
arguments.raw: -t,  --tunnel         Enable tunnel                             (default: false)

arguments.description: --ssl           Use TLS
arguments.raw: -s,     --ssl           Use TLS

arguments.description: --self-signed   Use a self-signed certificate
arguments.raw: -ss,    --self-signed   Use a self-signed certificate

arguments.description: --server-key    Path to server key
arguments.raw: -sk,    --server-key    Path to server key

arguments.description: --server-cert   Path to server certificate
arguments.raw: -sc,    --server-cert   Path to server certificate

arguments.description: --pkcs12        Path to server p12
arguments.raw: -p12,   --pkcs12        Path to server p12

arguments.description: Server p12 has empty password
arguments.raw: -p12np, --p12-no-pass   Server p12 has empty password

arguments.description: --lets-encrypt  Use Let's Encrypt as certification service
arguments.raw: -sl,    --lets-encrypt  Use Let's Encrypt as certification service

arguments.description: --le-domains    Domain(s) to request from Let's Encrypt	   (comma separated list)
arguments.raw: -sld,   --le-domains    Domain(s) to request from Let's Encrypt	   (comma separated list)

arguments.description: --le-email      Email to use with Let's Encrypt
arguments.raw: -sle,   --le-email      Email to use with Let's Encrypt

arguments.description: --le-http       Port to use for Let's Encrypt HTTP Challenge	   (default: 80)
arguments.raw: -slh,   --le-http       Port to use for Let's Encrypt HTTP Challenge	   (default: 80)

arguments.description: --le-tls        Port to use for Let's Encrypt TLS ALPN Challenge (default: 443)
arguments.raw: -slt,   --le-tls        Port to use for Let's Encrypt TLS ALPN Challenge (default: 443)

arguments.description: Activate FTP server capabilities          (default: false)
arguments.raw: -ftp                          Activate FTP server capabilities          (default: false)

arguments.description: The port FTP/SFTP listens on              (default: 2121)
arguments.raw: -ftp-port                     The port FTP/SFTP listens on              (default: 2121)

arguments.description: Switch to SFTP instead of plain FTP       (default: false)
arguments.raw: -ftp-sftp                     Switch to SFTP instead of plain FTP       (default: false)

arguments.description: Authorized_keys file for SFTP pubkey auth
arguments.raw: -fkf, --ftp-keyfile           Authorized_keys file for SFTP pubkey auth

arguments.description: SSH host key file for SFTP identification
arguments.raw: -fhk, --ftp-host-keyfile      SSH host key file for SFTP identification

arguments.description: Activate SMB server capabilities         (default: false)
arguments.raw: -smb                        Activate SMB server capabilities         (default: false)

arguments.description: --smb-port     The port SMB listens on                  (default: 445)
arguments.raw: -smb-port,   --smb-port     The port SMB listens on                  (default: 445)

arguments.description: The domain to use for SMB authentication (default: WORKGROUP)
arguments.raw: -smb-domain, --smb-domain   The domain to use for SMB authentication (default: WORKGROUP)

arguments.description: --smb-share    The share to use for SMB authentication  (default: goshs)
arguments.raw: -smb-share,  --smb-share    The share to use for SMB authentication  (default: goshs)

arguments.description: Wordlist file for quick hash cracking    (default: none)
arguments.raw: -smb-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick hash cracking    (default: none)

arguments.description: Activate LDAP credential capture server (default: false)
arguments.raw: -ldap, --ldap-server         Activate LDAP credential capture server (default: false)

arguments.description: The port LDAP listens on                 (default: 389)
arguments.raw: -ldap-port                   The port LDAP listens on                 (default: 389)

arguments.description: Enable dynamic JNDI mode — baseDN in the search becomes the
arguments.raw: -ldap-jndi                   Enable dynamic JNDI mode — baseDN in the search becomes the

arguments.description: Override codeBase URL for JNDI payloads  (default: auto)
arguments.raw: -ldap-jndi-base              Override codeBase URL for JNDI payloads  (default: auto)

arguments.description: Wordlist file for quick LDAP NTLM hash cracking
arguments.raw: -ldap-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick LDAP NTLM hash cracking

arguments.description: --basic-auth     Use basic authentication (user:pass - user can be empty)
arguments.raw: -b,  --basic-auth     Use basic authentication (user:pass - user can be empty)

arguments.description: Use certificate based authentication - provide ca certificate
arguments.raw: -ca, --cert-auth      Use certificate based authentication - provide ca certificate

arguments.description: --hash           Hash a password for file based ACLs
arguments.raw: -H,  --hash           Hash a password for file based ACLs

arguments.description: Comma separated list of IPs to whitelist
arguments.raw: -ipw, --ip-whitelist             Comma separated list of IPs to whitelist

arguments.description: Comma separated list of trusted proxies
arguments.raw: -tpw, --trusted-proxy-whitelist  Comma separated list of trusted proxies

arguments.description: Enable DNS server                   (default: false)
arguments.raw: -dns, --dns-server           Enable DNS server                   (default: false)

arguments.description: DNS server port                     (default: 8053)
arguments.raw: -dns-port, --dns-port        DNS server port                     (default: 8053)

arguments.description: DNS server Reply IP                 (default: 127.0.0.1)
arguments.raw: -dns-ip, --dns-ip            DNS server Reply IP                 (default: 127.0.0.1)

arguments.description: Enable SMTP server                  (default: false)
arguments.raw: -smtp, --smtp-server         Enable SMTP server                  (default: false)

arguments.description: SMTP server port                    (default: 2525)
arguments.raw: -smtp-port, --smtp-port      SMTP server port                    (default: 2525)

arguments.description: SMTP server domain                  (default: open relay)
arguments.raw: -smtp-domain, --smtp-domain  SMTP server domain                  (default: open relay)

arguments.description: --webhook            Enable webhook support                      (default: false)
arguments.raw: -W,  --webhook            Enable webhook support                      (default: false)

arguments.description: URL to send webhook requests to
arguments.raw: -Wu, --webhook-url        URL to send webhook requests to

arguments.description: Comma separated list of events to notify
arguments.raw: -We, --webhook-events     Comma separated list of events to notify

arguments.description: Webhook provider
arguments.raw: -Wp, --webhook-provider   Webhook provider

arguments.description: --config        Provide config file path                (default: false)
arguments.raw: -C  --config        Provide config file path                (default: false)

arguments.description: --print-config  Print sample config to STDOUT           (default: false)
arguments.raw: -P  --print-config  Print sample config to STDOUT           (default: false)

arguments.description: --user          Drop privs to user (unix only)          (default: current user)
arguments.raw: -u  --user          Drop privs to user (unix only)          (default: current user)

arguments.description: Update goshs to most recent version
arguments.raw: --update        Update goshs to most recent version

arguments.description: --mdns          Enable zeroconf mDNS registration       (default: false)
arguments.raw: -m  --mdns          Enable zeroconf mDNS registration       (default: false)

arguments.description: --verbose       Activate verbose log output             (default: false)
arguments.raw: -V  --verbose       Activate verbose log output             (default: false)

arguments.description: Print the current goshs version
arguments.raw: -v                  Print the current goshs version

arguments.description: Install shell tab completion (bash|fish|zsh)
arguments.raw: --completion    Install shell tab completion (bash|fish|zsh)

arguments.description: Activate TFTP server capabilities         (default: false)
arguments.raw: -tftp, --tftp-server          Activate TFTP server capabilities         (default: false)

arguments.description: The port TFTP listens on                  (default: 69)
arguments.raw: -tftp-port                    The port TFTP listens on                  (default: 69)

arguments.description: Render {{.VAR}} placeholders in files requested with ?tpl (default: false)
arguments.raw: --template      Render {{.VAR}} placeholders in files requested with ?tpl (default: false)

arguments.description: Template variable KEY=VALUE (repeatable), e.g. LPORT=4444
arguments.raw: --tpl-var       Template variable KEY=VALUE (repeatable), e.g. LPORT=4444

arguments.description: Run the interactive terminal dashboard  (default: false)
arguments.raw: --tui           Run the interactive terminal dashboard  (default: false)

arguments.description: Self-destruct after a duration, e.g. 30m, 2h (default: disabled)
arguments.raw: --ttl           Self-destruct after a duration, e.g. 30m, 2h (default: disabled)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# goshs -h
 
 goshs v2.1.0
 Usage: goshs [options]
 
 Web server options:
 -i,  --ip             IP or Interface to listen on              (default: 0.0.0.0)
 -p,  --port           The port to listen on                     (default: 8000)
 -d,  --dir            The web root directory                    (default: current working path)
 -w,  --webdav         Also serve using webdav protocol          (default: false)
 -wp, --webdav-port    The port to listen on for webdav          (default: 8001)
 -ro, --read-only      Read only mode, no upload possible        (default: false)
 -uo, --upload-only    Upload only mode, no download possible    (default: false)
 -uf, --upload-folder  Specify a different upload folder         (default: current working path)
 -mu, --max-upload     Maximum upload size in bytes, 0=unlimited (default: 0)
 -nc, --no-clipboard   Disable the clipboard sharing             (default: false)
 -nd, --no-delete      Disable the delete option                 (default: false)
 -si, --silent         Running without dir listing               (default: false)
 -I,  --invisible      Invisible mode                            (default: false)
 -c,  --cli            Enable cli (only with auth and tls)       (default: false)
 --catcher, -rc        Enable reverse shell catcher              (default: false)
 -e,  --embedded       Show embedded files in UI                 (default: false)
 -o,  --output         Write output to logfile                   (default: false)
 -t,  --tunnel         Enable tunnel                             (default: false)
 
 TLS options:
 -s,     --ssl           Use TLS
 -ss,    --self-signed   Use a self-signed certificate
 -sk,    --server-key    Path to server key
 -sc,    --server-cert   Path to server certificate
 -p12,   --pkcs12        Path to server p12
 -p12np, --p12-no-pass   Server p12 has empty password
 -sl,    --lets-encrypt  Use Let's Encrypt as certification service
 -sld,   --le-domains    Domain(s) to request from Let's Encrypt	   (comma separated list)
 -sle,   --le-email      Email to use with Let's Encrypt
 -slh,   --le-http       Port to use for Let's Encrypt HTTP Challenge	   (default: 80)
 -slt,   --le-tls        Port to use for Let's Encrypt TLS ALPN Challenge (default: 443)
 
 FTP/SFTP server options:
 -ftp                          Activate FTP server capabilities          (default: false)
 -ftp-port                     The port FTP/SFTP listens on              (default: 2121)
 -ftp-sftp                     Switch to SFTP instead of plain FTP       (default: false)
 -fkf, --ftp-keyfile           Authorized_keys file for SFTP pubkey auth
 -fhk, --ftp-host-keyfile      SSH host key file for SFTP identification
 
 SMB server options:
 -smb                        Activate SMB server capabilities         (default: false)
 -smb-port,   --smb-port     The port SMB listens on                  (default: 445)
 -smb-domain, --smb-domain   The domain to use for SMB authentication (default: WORKGROUP)
 -smb-share,  --smb-share    The share to use for SMB authentication  (default: goshs)
 -smb-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick hash cracking    (default: none)
 
 LDAP server options:
 -ldap, --ldap-server         Activate LDAP credential capture server (default: false)
 -ldap-port                   The port LDAP listens on                 (default: 389)
 -ldap-jndi                   Enable dynamic JNDI mode — baseDN in the search becomes the
                              factory class name, fetched from the goshs HTTP server
 -ldap-jndi-base              Override codeBase URL for JNDI payloads  (default: auto)
 -ldap-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick LDAP NTLM hash cracking
 Use -s -ss or -s -sc/-sk to enable LDAPS (TLS) on default port 636
 
 Authentication options:
 -b,  --basic-auth     Use basic authentication (user:pass - user can be empty)
 -ca, --cert-auth      Use certificate based authentication - provide ca certificate
 -H,  --hash           Hash a password for file based ACLs
 
 Connection restriction:
 -ipw, --ip-whitelist             Comma separated list of IPs to whitelist
 -tpw, --trusted-proxy-whitelist  Comma separated list of trusted proxies
 
 Collaboration options:
 -dns, --dns-server           Enable DNS server                   (default: false)
 -dns-port, --dns-port        DNS server port                     (default: 8053)
 -dns-ip, --dns-ip            DNS server Reply IP                 (default: 127.0.0.1)
 -smtp, --smtp-server         Enable SMTP server                  (default: false)
 -smtp-port, --smtp-port      SMTP server port                    (default: 2525)
 -smtp-domain, --smtp-domain  SMTP server domain                  (default: open relay)
 
 Webhook options:
 -W,  --webhook            Enable webhook support                      (default: false)
 -Wu, --webhook-url        URL to send webhook requests to
 -We, --webhook-events     Comma separated list of events to notify
                           [all, upload, delete, download, view, webdav,
                           ftp, smb, dns, smtp, redirect, verbose]	    (default: all)
 -Wp, --webhook-provider   Webhook provider
                           [Discord, Mattermost, Slack]                (default: Discord)
 
 Misc options:
 -C  --config        Provide config file path                (default: false)
 -P  --print-config  Print sample config to STDOUT           (default: false)
 -u  --user          Drop privs to user (unix only)          (default: current user)
     --update        Update goshs to most recent version
 -m  --mdns          Enable zeroconf mDNS registration       (default: false)
 -V  --verbose       Activate verbose log output             (default: false)
 -v                  Print the current goshs version
 
     --completion    Install shell tab completion (bash|fish|zsh)
 
 Usage examples:
 Start with default values:    	./goshs
 Start with config file:    	        ./goshs -C /path/to/config.yaml
 Start with wevdav support:    	./goshs -w
 Start with different port:    	./goshs -p 8080
 Start with self-signed cert:  	./goshs -s -ss
 Start with let's encrypt:		./goshs -s -sl -sle your@mail.com -sld your.domain.com,your.seconddomain.com
 Start with custom cert:       	./goshs -s -sk <path to key> -sc <path to cert>
 Start with basic auth:        	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$up3r$3cur3'
 Start with basic auth bcrypt hash:   	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$2a$14$ydRJ//Ob4SctB/D7o.rvU.LmPs/vwXkeXCbtpCqzgOJDSShLgiY52'
 Start with basic auth empty user:	./goshs -b ':$up3r$3cur3'
 Start with cli enabled:           	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$up3r$3cur3' -s -ss -c

local_help.--help:
goshs v2.1.3
Usage: goshs [options]

Web server options:
  -i,  --ip             IP or Interface to listen on              (default: 0.0.0.0)
  -p,  --port           The port to listen on                     (default: 8000)
  -d,  --dir            The web root directory                    (default: current working path)
  -w,  --webdav         Also serve using webdav protocol          (default: false)
  -wp, --webdav-port    The port to listen on for webdav          (default: 8001)
  -ro, --read-only      Read only mode, no upload possible        (default: false)
  -uo, --upload-only    Upload only mode, no download possible    (default: false)
  -uf, --upload-folder  Specify a different upload folder         (default: current working path)
  -mu, --max-upload     Maximum upload size in bytes, 0=unlimited (default: 0)
  -nc, --no-clipboard   Disable the clipboard sharing             (default: false)
  -nd, --no-delete      Disable the delete option                 (default: false)
  -si, --silent         Running without dir listing               (default: false)
  -I,  --invisible      Invisible mode                            (default: false)
  -c,  --cli            Enable cli (only with auth and tls)       (default: false)
  --catcher, -rc        Enable reverse shell catcher              (default: false)
  -e,  --embedded       Show embedded files in UI                 (default: false)
  -o,  --output         Write output to logfile                   (default: false)
  -t,  --tunnel         Enable tunnel                             (default: false)

TLS options:
  -s,     --ssl           Use TLS
  -ss,    --self-signed   Use a self-signed certificate
  -sk,    --server-key    Path to server key
  -sc,    --server-cert   Path to server certificate
  -p12,   --pkcs12        Path to server p12
  -p12np, --p12-no-pass   Server p12 has empty password
  -sl,    --lets-encrypt  Use Let's Encrypt as certification service
  -sld,   --le-domains    Domain(s) to request from Let's Encrypt	   (comma separated list)
  -sle,   --le-email      Email to use with Let's Encrypt
  -slh,   --le-http       Port to use for Let's Encrypt HTTP Challenge	   (default: 80)
  -slt,   --le-tls        Port to use for Let's Encrypt TLS ALPN Challenge (default: 443)

FTP/SFTP server options:
  -ftp                          Activate FTP server capabilities          (default: false)
  -ftp-port                     The port FTP/SFTP listens on              (default: 2121)
  -ftp-sftp                     Switch to SFTP instead of plain FTP       (default: false)
  -fkf, --ftp-keyfile           Authorized_keys file for SFTP pubkey auth
  -fhk, --ftp-host-keyfile      SSH host key file for SFTP identification

TFTP server options:
  -tftp, --tftp-server          Activate TFTP server capabilities         (default: false)
  -tftp-port                    The port TFTP listens on                  (default: 69)

SMB server options:
  -smb                        Activate SMB server capabilities         (default: false)
  -smb-port,   --smb-port     The port SMB listens on                  (default: 445)
  -smb-domain, --smb-domain   The domain to use for SMB authentication (default: WORKGROUP)
  -smb-share,  --smb-share    The share to use for SMB authentication  (default: goshs)
  -smb-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick hash cracking    (default: none)

LDAP server options:
  -ldap, --ldap-server         Activate LDAP credential capture server (default: false)
  -ldap-port                   The port LDAP listens on                 (default: 389)
  -ldap-jndi                   Enable dynamic JNDI mode — baseDN in the search becomes the
                               factory class name, fetched from the goshs HTTP server
  -ldap-jndi-base              Override codeBase URL for JNDI payloads  (default: auto)
  -ldap-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick LDAP NTLM hash cracking
  Use -s -ss or -s -sc/-sk to enable LDAPS (TLS) on default port 636

Authentication options:
  -b,  --basic-auth     Use basic authentication (user:pass - user can be empty)
  -ca, --cert-auth      Use certificate based authentication - provide ca certificate
  -H,  --hash           Hash a password for file based ACLs

Connection restriction:
  -ipw, --ip-whitelist             Comma separated list of IPs to whitelist
  -tpw, --trusted-proxy-whitelist  Comma separated list of trusted proxies

Collaboration options:
  -dns, --dns-server           Enable DNS server                   (default: false)
  -dns-port, --dns-port        DNS server port                     (default: 8053)
  -dns-ip, --dns-ip            DNS server Reply IP                 (default: 127.0.0.1)
  -smtp, --smtp-server         Enable SMTP server                  (default: false)
  -smtp-port, --smtp-port      SMTP server port                    (default: 2525)
  -smtp-domain, --smtp-domain  SMTP server domain                  (default: open relay)

Webhook options:
  -W,  --webhook            Enable webhook support                      (default: false)
  -Wu, --webhook-url        URL to send webhook requests to
  -We, --webhook-events     Comma separated list of events to notify
                            [all, upload, delete, download, view, webdav,
                            ftp, smb, dns, smtp, redirect, verbose]	    (default: all)
  -Wp, --webhook-provider   Webhook provider
                            [Discord, Mattermost, Slack]                (default: Discord)

Payload templating:
      --template      Render {{.VAR}} placeholders in files requested with ?tpl (default: false)
      --tpl-var       Template variable KEY=VALUE (repeatable), e.g. LPORT=4444
                      LHOST defaults to -i when a single IP is bound; on 0.0.0.0 pass --tpl-var LHOST=

Misc options:
  -C  --config        Provide config file path                (default: false)
  -P  --print-config  Print sample config to STDOUT           (default: false)
  -u  --user          Drop privs to user (unix only)          (default: current user)
      --update        Update goshs to most recent version
  -m  --mdns          Enable zeroconf mDNS registration       (default: false)
      --tui           Run the interactive terminal dashboard  (default: false)
      --ttl           Self-destruct after a duration, e.g. 30m, 2h (default: disabled)
  -V  --verbose       Activate verbose log output             (default: false)
  -v                  Print the current goshs version

      --completion    Install shell tab completion (bash|fish|zsh)

Usage examples:
  Start with default values:    	./goshs
  Start with config file:    	        ./goshs -C /path/to/config.yaml
  Start with wevdav support:    	./goshs -w
  Start with different port:    	./goshs -p 8080
  Start with self-signed cert:  	./goshs -s -ss
  Start with let's encrypt:		./goshs -s -sl -sle your@mail.com -sld your.domain.com,your.seconddomain.com
  Start with custom cert:       	./goshs -s -sk <path to key> -sc <path to cert>
  Start with basic auth:        	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$up3r$3cur3'
  Start with basic auth bcrypt hash:   	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$2a$14$ydRJ//Ob4SctB/D7o.rvU.LmPs/vwXkeXCbtpCqzgOJDSShLgiY52'
  Start with basic auth empty user:	./goshs -b ':$up3r$3cur3'
  Start with cli enabled:           	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$up3r$3cur3' -s -ss -c
  Start with payload templating:    	./goshs -i 10.10.14.7 --template --tpl-var LPORT=4444
local_help.-h:
goshs v2.1.3
Usage: goshs [options]

Web server options:
  -i,  --ip             IP or Interface to listen on              (default: 0.0.0.0)
  -p,  --port           The port to listen on                     (default: 8000)
  -d,  --dir            The web root directory                    (default: current working path)
  -w,  --webdav         Also serve using webdav protocol          (default: false)
  -wp, --webdav-port    The port to listen on for webdav          (default: 8001)
  -ro, --read-only      Read only mode, no upload possible        (default: false)
  -uo, --upload-only    Upload only mode, no download possible    (default: false)
  -uf, --upload-folder  Specify a different upload folder         (default: current working path)
  -mu, --max-upload     Maximum upload size in bytes, 0=unlimited (default: 0)
  -nc, --no-clipboard   Disable the clipboard sharing             (default: false)
  -nd, --no-delete      Disable the delete option                 (default: false)
  -si, --silent         Running without dir listing               (default: false)
  -I,  --invisible      Invisible mode                            (default: false)
  -c,  --cli            Enable cli (only with auth and tls)       (default: false)
  --catcher, -rc        Enable reverse shell catcher              (default: false)
  -e,  --embedded       Show embedded files in UI                 (default: false)
  -o,  --output         Write output to logfile                   (default: false)
  -t,  --tunnel         Enable tunnel                             (default: false)

TLS options:
  -s,     --ssl           Use TLS
  -ss,    --self-signed   Use a self-signed certificate
  -sk,    --server-key    Path to server key
  -sc,    --server-cert   Path to server certificate
  -p12,   --pkcs12        Path to server p12
  -p12np, --p12-no-pass   Server p12 has empty password
  -sl,    --lets-encrypt  Use Let's Encrypt as certification service
  -sld,   --le-domains    Domain(s) to request from Let's Encrypt	   (comma separated list)
  -sle,   --le-email      Email to use with Let's Encrypt
  -slh,   --le-http       Port to use for Let's Encrypt HTTP Challenge	   (default: 80)
  -slt,   --le-tls        Port to use for Let's Encrypt TLS ALPN Challenge (default: 443)

FTP/SFTP server options:
  -ftp                          Activate FTP server capabilities          (default: false)
  -ftp-port                     The port FTP/SFTP listens on              (default: 2121)
  -ftp-sftp                     Switch to SFTP instead of plain FTP       (default: false)
  -fkf, --ftp-keyfile           Authorized_keys file for SFTP pubkey auth
  -fhk, --ftp-host-keyfile      SSH host key file for SFTP identification

TFTP server options:
  -tftp, --tftp-server          Activate TFTP server capabilities         (default: false)
  -tftp-port                    The port TFTP listens on                  (default: 69)

SMB server options:
  -smb                        Activate SMB server capabilities         (default: false)
  -smb-port,   --smb-port     The port SMB listens on                  (default: 445)
  -smb-domain, --smb-domain   The domain to use for SMB authentication (default: WORKGROUP)
  -smb-share,  --smb-share    The share to use for SMB authentication  (default: goshs)
  -smb-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick hash cracking    (default: none)

LDAP server options:
  -ldap, --ldap-server         Activate LDAP credential capture server (default: false)
  -ldap-port                   The port LDAP listens on                 (default: 389)
  -ldap-jndi                   Enable dynamic JNDI mode — baseDN in the search becomes the
                               factory class name, fetched from the goshs HTTP server
  -ldap-jndi-base              Override codeBase URL for JNDI payloads  (default: auto)
  -ldap-wordlist               Wordlist file for quick LDAP NTLM hash cracking
  Use -s -ss or -s -sc/-sk to enable LDAPS (TLS) on default port 636

Authentication options:
  -b,  --basic-auth     Use basic authentication (user:pass - user can be empty)
  -ca, --cert-auth      Use certificate based authentication - provide ca certificate
  -H,  --hash           Hash a password for file based ACLs

Connection restriction:
  -ipw, --ip-whitelist             Comma separated list of IPs to whitelist
  -tpw, --trusted-proxy-whitelist  Comma separated list of trusted proxies

Collaboration options:
  -dns, --dns-server           Enable DNS server                   (default: false)
  -dns-port, --dns-port        DNS server port                     (default: 8053)
  -dns-ip, --dns-ip            DNS server Reply IP                 (default: 127.0.0.1)
  -smtp, --smtp-server         Enable SMTP server                  (default: false)
  -smtp-port, --smtp-port      SMTP server port                    (default: 2525)
  -smtp-domain, --smtp-domain  SMTP server domain                  (default: open relay)

Webhook options:
  -W,  --webhook            Enable webhook support                      (default: false)
  -Wu, --webhook-url        URL to send webhook requests to
  -We, --webhook-events     Comma separated list of events to notify
                            [all, upload, delete, download, view, webdav,
                            ftp, smb, dns, smtp, redirect, verbose]	    (default: all)
  -Wp, --webhook-provider   Webhook provider
                            [Discord, Mattermost, Slack]                (default: Discord)

Payload templating:
      --template      Render {{.VAR}} placeholders in files requested with ?tpl (default: false)
      --tpl-var       Template variable KEY=VALUE (repeatable), e.g. LPORT=4444
                      LHOST defaults to -i when a single IP is bound; on 0.0.0.0 pass --tpl-var LHOST=

Misc options:
  -C  --config        Provide config file path                (default: false)
  -P  --print-config  Print sample config to STDOUT           (default: false)
  -u  --user          Drop privs to user (unix only)          (default: current user)
      --update        Update goshs to most recent version
  -m  --mdns          Enable zeroconf mDNS registration       (default: false)
      --tui           Run the interactive terminal dashboard  (default: false)
      --ttl           Self-destruct after a duration, e.g. 30m, 2h (default: disabled)
  -V  --verbose       Activate verbose log output             (default: false)
  -v                  Print the current goshs version

      --completion    Install shell tab completion (bash|fish|zsh)

Usage examples:
  Start with default values:    	./goshs
  Start with config file:    	        ./goshs -C /path/to/config.yaml
  Start with wevdav support:    	./goshs -w
  Start with different port:    	./goshs -p 8080
  Start with self-signed cert:  	./goshs -s -ss
  Start with let's encrypt:		./goshs -s -sl -sle your@mail.com -sld your.domain.com,your.seconddomain.com
  Start with custom cert:       	./goshs -s -sk <path to key> -sc <path to cert>
  Start with basic auth:        	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$up3r$3cur3'
  Start with basic auth bcrypt hash:   	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$2a$14$ydRJ//Ob4SctB/D7o.rvU.LmPs/vwXkeXCbtpCqzgOJDSShLgiY52'
  Start with basic auth empty user:	./goshs -b ':$up3r$3cur3'
  Start with cli enabled:           	./goshs -b 'secret-user:$up3r$3cur3' -s -ss -c
  Start with payload templating:    	./goshs -i 10.10.14.7 --template --tpl-var LPORT=4444

apt_metadata.description:
SimpleHTTPServer written in Go
This tool provides a SimpleHTTPServer written in Go, enhanced with features
and security. This package contains a simple http server like the Python
SimpleHTTPServer but enhanced with a lot of helpful features and security in
mind.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
